author: Alice
text: React interviews love the nested comments problem. It really tests how you think about recursion.

children.author: Bob
children.text: Agreed. The tricky part is updating a deeply nested node without mutating state.

children.author: Alice
children.text: Exactly. A recursive helper that returns a new array at each level does the job cleanly.

children.author: Carol
children.text: Don't forget that deleting a comment should also remove its whole subtree.

author: Dave
text: A single source of truth at the top with one-way data flow keeps this manageable.
